We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a) the Municipal Mayor:
i. in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, make a formal request with
the Commission on Audit for the writing-off of dormant unliquidated cash
advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008; and
ii. direct the concerned officers and employees still connected with the agency to
immediately submit their liquidation papers to settle their unliquidated
cash advances, otherwise enforce the withholding of salary.
b) the Municipal Accountant:
i. issue regular demand letters to AOs with unliquidated cash advances for
special purposes and travel expenses, copy furnished the COA Auditor, in
preparation for the issuance of the auditor’s final demand letter;
ii. in coordination with the Municipal Mayor, formulate a written internal
control policy on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances
guided by the laws, rules and regulations discussed herein;
iii. stop granting additional cash advances to any official or employee with
existing unliquidated cash advance;
iv. prepare and attach in the cash advance vouchers the required certification
that previous unliquidated cash advances have been fully liquidated; and
v. enforce the withholding of salary of those officers and employees still
employed by the LGU with outstanding cash advances, particularly those
recently granted, to compel liquidation thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
a) the Municipal Mayor together with the Local Finance Committee:
i. adopt urgently appropriate control measures to eliminate cash deficits
pursuant to Section 337 of RA No. 7160;
ii. review income budgeting procedures as well as revenue generation
capacities in order to establish more realizable income estimates and
maximize revenue collection, as the case may be; and
iii. secure the passage of necessary ordinances prior to incorporating
proposed revenues in the annual budget.
b) the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer coordinate accordingly in monitoring the actual revenue and
disbursements in order to avoid incurring obligations and having capital
outlay appropriations without sufficient appropriations and funds.
Management’s comment
As discussed during the audit exit conference and as stated in their written
response, Management expressed concurrence with the audit observations and affirmed
their commitment to implement the corresponding audit recommendations. In line with
this, for Calendar Year 2025, subsequent revenue projections were prepared based solely
on sources with verified bases and a high probability of collection. Moreover, as of the
second quarter, the LGU has already achieved its revenue targets, indicating improved
accuracy in revenue forecasting and enhanced collection efficiency.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
a) the Municipal Accountant:
i. evaluate the possibility of hiring additional personnel for the
Accounting Office to address manpower limitations, ease workload
pressures, and allow sufficient time for the timely preparation and
submission of FTDs to this Office;
ii. explore the use of electronic signatures in affixing approvals on Journal
Entry Vouchers and transmittal letters to COA, in accordance with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2021-00611 dated September 6, 2021;
iii. enhance the existing filing and records management system for FTDs to
ensure proper segregation, ease of retrieval, and to minimize the
occurrence of missing documents; and
iv. strictly comply with the prescribed timelines for submission of FTDs to
this Office in all succeeding periods.
b) the Municipal Mayor adopt and enforce the suspension of salary payments to
the officials concerned in cases of failure to submit the required documents
for audit, in accordance with Section 122(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
Management’s comment
During the audit exit conference and as stated in their written response,
Management concurred with the audit observations and affirmed their commitment to
implement the corresponding audit recommendations. Additionally, Management gave
assurance to the COA Audit Team that all required financial transaction documents
would be submitted promptly to ensure timely and proper review, validation, and
evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management
agreed on the following:
1. For the Municipal General Services Officer to:
a. conduct a comprehensive physical count of Property, Plant and
Equipment items of the Municipality following the inventory taking
procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the
One-Time Cleansing of PPE Accounts; and
b. conduct annual separate inventory-taking and report for all furniture,
equipment, books and work animal costing below ₱10,000.00 (semi-
expendable items) and small tangible items.
2. For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. review the PPE list to identify items needed to be excluded from the PPE
accounts relative to the small tangible items and semi-expendable items;
b. make proper adjustments in the books on identified capitalized small
tangible items and semi-expendable items; and
c. coordinate with the MGSO for the conduct of physical inventory count,
updating of inventory reports/records and reconciliation thereof.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management
agreed on the following:
1. For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. ensure immediate settlement of the PDAF & CDF balances by remitting it
to the National Treasury in light of the Supreme Court decision taking
into consideration the necessary documentation / details thereof;
b. refund unexpended/excess project funds, other than PDAF and those
agreed to be reprogrammed, to the respective source agency or otherwise
request for use in other appropriate activities, whichever is applicable, in
accordance with the agreement and terms of grant; and
c. together with the Municipal Treasurer, determine the nature/purpose of
funds duly receipted in Trust Fund without known specific purpose of the
grant.
2. For the Local Chief Executive to direct the Heads of the implementing
departments (Municipal Engineering Office, Municipal Agriculturist Office,
Rural Health Unit, MSWDO, etc.), in coordination with the Municipal
Accountant, to implement promptly the corresponding PPAs funded by
unutilized grants and ensure the timely liquidation thereof.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management
agreed for the Municipal Development Council to:
a. stop the practice of charging to 20% development fund expenditures that are
administrative in nature and do not partake the nature of expense that are
essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people in consonance
with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 to ensure optimal
utilization of the fund; and
b. revert unimplemented ineligible projects for re-appropriation to PPAs
identified under the abovementioned JMC.

agreed on the following:

b. henceforth, ensure that any unexpended balances of DRRM-MOOE is
transferred to the Trust Fund at the end of the current year.
2. For the Local Finance Committee to:
a. stop the practice of re-alignment/augmentation of appropriations without
appropriate legal basis and, henceforth, make certain that it shall be
made in accordance with Sections 322 or 336 of the RA No. 7160, as the
case may be, and in line with other applicable laws, rules and regulations.

LDRRMFIP before the release and use of funds therein in accordance
with Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

observations.recommendation:
1. The Sangguniang Bayan to:
a. ensure that only eligible PPAs receive appropriation under the 20% LDF in accordance with DILG – DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and
b. ensure that pertinent reports on PPAs are submitted to COA timely and regularly as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
2. The Municipal Development Council to:
a. refrain from recommending ineligible PPAs to be funded by the 20% LDF; and
b. provide bases for the noted changes in appropriation, particularly some of the COVID-19-related appropriations including all changes in continuing appropriations.
3. The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to:
a. reconcile regularly and coordinate accordingly with respect to preparation of inter-related reports used across different departments, in accordance with pertinent regulations, objectively and accurately; and
b. refrain from granting cash advances for payments on account of infrastructure projects or other undertaking on a project basis.
4. The Municipal Accountant to:
a. provide and attach to the DV the Certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting thereof in conformity with existing rules and regulations;
b. submit all lacking DVs and its supporting documents noted in the above-mentioned observations; and
c. henceforth, ensure that the monthly transaction documents of the LGU are submitted completely and on a timely basis to COA.
5. The Local Chief Executive to:
a. regularly enforce and monitor the implementation of eligible LDF-PPAs in order to ensure that the LDF are optimally utilized; and
b. ensure that pertinent reports on PPAs are promptly submitted to COA completely.

observations.recommendation:
With the above observations, we recommended and management agreed on the
following:
1. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a. substantiate, duly supported with relevant and appropriate reports for the
unexplained discrepancy of P844,959.90 between Bank Outstanding Balance
and Adjusted Loans Payable Account, and effect the necessary adjustment
supported with Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), if necessary;
b. coordinate accordingly with the respective depository banks and provide
explanations for the noted discrepancies in the DBP and LBP Amortization
Schedules and Debit Loan Payments duly supported with relevant
reports/records; and
c. henceforth, intensify reconciliation of pertinent loan records and reports, and
observe proper coordination regularly between the accounting and treasury
departments on matters relating to loans, in order to detect and rectify errors
immediately.
2. The Local Finance Committee, together with the Members of the Sangguniang
Bayan, to exercise due diligence in evaluating the contents of the budget
proposals and ensure that all lawful expenditures and obligations, particularly
loan amortizations, obtain sufficient and available appropriations in accordance
with Section 305 and 336 of RA 7160.
3. The Municipal Accountant to request the LGU’s Accounting System Provider
(ECPAC) to provide Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) for each and every loan account,
maintain its reconciliation with pertinent reports and records and ensure that its
purpose is maximized.

With the above observations, we recommended and management agreed on the
following:
1. The Municipal Accountant and the General Service Office to:
a. settle immediately the discrepancies between the two reports and effect the
required adjustments in their respective records, and in the case of accounting-
record-reconciling-items, corresponding journal vouchers shall be prepared and
effected by the Municipal Accountant; and
b. henceforth, regularly reconcile the PPE balances between the RPCPPEs and
accounting records so as to determine promptly the discrepancies between the
records.
2. The Municipal Mayor to consider evaluating the urgency and practicability of
manpower augmentation through hiring of additional personnel either or both in
the Accounting and General Services Offices, or otherwise special-undertaking
manpower reinforcement through temporary designation of available and capable
LGU employee(s) in inventory team and/or PPE accounts-reconciliation team.

With the foregoing observations, we reiterated amd management agreed on the
following audit recommendations:
1. The Municipal Accountant to henceforth, take up all lawful expenditures and
obligations incurred/paid, in their respective accounts, within the year of its
occurrence.
2. The Local Finance Committee (LFC) to:
a. ensure sufficiency/existence of appropriation for all lawful obligations,
particularly those covered with Amortization Schedules and Approved
Contracts, including costs of checkbooks, pursuant to all applicable laws.
b. to refrain from resorting to availment of additional loan(s) in financing PPAs
especially when the financial capacity of the LGU to repay does not permit.
3. The Budget Officer to make certain that expenditures are charged exclusively
against the approved budget pertaining thereto in order to avoid distorting the
related appropriations intended for other PPAs of the current and the ensuing
year/s.

With the above observations, we recommended and management agreed on the
following:

unserviceable properties and initiate the disposal thereof; and

receivables resulting from unrefunded loss, missing and stolen PPE items,
including the death of Biological Assets, after necessary reconciliation of
corresponding account balances; and
b. coordinate with the GSO in the reconciliation of discrepancies in PPE
accounts, and thereafter, effect necessary journal entries, if any.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
1. intensify liquidation of cash advances by consistently and regularly issuing
demand letters to the concerned officers and employees, both active and
inactive, through the responsible Accounting Staff in-charge of the cash
advances;
2. refrain from further granting additional cash advance/s to officials/employees
with outstanding advances prior to liquidation of their respective cash advance
balances, in line with Section 89 of PD 1445, among others;
3. expedite evaluation of complied liquidation reports with respect to the long
outstanding cash advances for Confidential Fund and thereafter effect the
liquidation thereof by derecognizing in the books the corresponding amount in
accordance with COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001
dated January 8, 2015; and
4. henceforth, adhere strictly with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations in the
granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances in order to safeguard the
funds of the local government, recognize all expenses within the applicable
fiscal year and thus present fairly the financial statements.

accounting records so as to determine promptly the discrepancies between
the records; and

and in the case of accounting-record-reconciling-items, corresponding
journal vouchers shall be prepared and effected by the Municipal
Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned official rectify this accounting practice in order to present fairly the financial statements. Also, correcting entries should be drawn in order to reflect the appropriate balances of the accounts affected. Strict compliance to Section 114 and Section 121 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Unit (LGU), Volume I is also advised.
The Municipal Accountant should review “Subsidy to/from other funds” accounts, and prepare necessary reconciliations and adjusting entries accordingly.

The management should instruct the collecting officers to turn-over their collections to the Municipal Treasurer for subsequent deposit not later than the next banking day in compliance with PD 1445, Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs and paragraphs 32 and 33 of the Revised Cash Examination Manual. Also, measures should be instituted to ensure compliance with the aforementioned regulations.

We recommended that the management and other officials concerned closely monitor the utilization and implementation of 20% Development Fund to maximize the purpose of the fund. The implementing department concerned should fast track the implementation based on the scheduled time frames to achieve the desired outcomes of the management. Strict compliance to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the Department of Interior and Local Government and Department of Budget and Management is advised.

We recommended that the Management and other concerned officials closely monitor the disbursements and bank transactions of the Municipality to avoid any incurrence of negative Cash balances and to follow the provision as stated in the Local Government Code.

The management through the Municipal Accountant shall closely monitor the status of net pay of their employees. Payroll clerks should be instructed to follow the prioritization on authorized deductions so the net pay will not fall below P3,500.00. Likewise, strict compliance with the provisions of Section 52 of the General Provisions of General Appropriations Act of 2016 is highly enjoined.

Create an Inventory Team that will conduct a complete physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment owned by the Municipality to ascertain the existence, ownership and validity of the PPE accounts. The Inventory Team, upon completion of inventory should prepare the prescribed RPCPPE, and reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Conduct physical count of the property, plant and equipment owned by the municipality to reconcile the amount stated on the books and with the actual existence of those assets.
observations.reason: The General Services Office conduct an on-going physical count. They are on the process of finalization of the reports. Completed reports will then be compared with the PPE schedule recorded by the Accounting Office to check the accuracy of the PPE amounts.
